slides: A video that was never filmed

HeyGen's Hyperframes converts HTML and CSS directly into MP4 video files. No browser recording, no screen capture, no rendering pipeline. The code becomes the video. Deterministically, pixel-perfect, every time.
This was built specifically for AI agents. An agent writes HTML, runs Hyperframes, and gets back a video file. No human opens a browser. No GUI interaction. Pure code-to-video generation.
Marketing agencies currently charge $3,000-15,000 for animated product videos. Hyperframes can generate the same output from a template and data feed in seconds. The cost drops to nearly zero.

The real disruption: dynamic video at scale. Personalized videos for 10,000 customers, each with different names, products, and offers, generated from a single HTML template. Try doing that in After Effects.
Static images and text are losing the attention war. Video converts 2-3x better across every platform. Hyperframes makes video the default output format, not a premium add-on.
Follow for more on AI media tools
posts.caption: HTML + CSS = MP4. No camera, no editor, no rendering pipeline. AI agents just got a video factory. How would you use code-to-video? Follow for more on AI media tools.

posts.topic: The agency model is cracking and most do not see it
posts.content:
A $50,000 animated video project now costs under $200 to produce.

I am not exaggerating. I have seen three companies replace their entire motion graphics pipeline with AI-powered HTML-to-video tools in the last 60 days.

The agency model was built on two things: creative talent and production labor. AI is eating the production labor part at a pace that would have seemed absurd 12 months ago. What used to require a designer, an animator, a compositor, and a render farm now requires a prompt and a template.

The agencies that will survive are not the ones with the best software. They are the ones with the best strategic thinking. Clients do not need you to make a video anymore. They need you to decide which video to make, for whom, and why.

That is a consulting business, not a production business.

The transition will be brutal for agencies that cannot reposition. But for solo strategists who understand AI production tools, this is the widest-open market I have seen in a decade.

Are you selling production or strategy?

posts.topic: Static content is dead and most brands are still writing blog posts
posts.content:
Brands are pouring money into blog content that nobody reads while video outperforms text by 3-5x on every engagement metric.

I analyzed 200 B2B content strategies last quarter. The ones growing fastest had one thing in common: video as the primary format, text as the supporting format.

The old rule was "write a blog post, maybe make a video if budget allows." The new rule is "make a video first, extract text from it."

The reason this is now possible is that AI has collapsed the cost of video production to near zero. HTML-to-video frameworks mean a developer can generate marketing videos from data templates without ever opening editing software.

The brands that figure this out in 2026 will have a 12-18 month head start on their competitors. The ones still investing in 2,000-word blog posts as their primary content format will wonder why their traffic flatlined.

The audience already moved.

What percentage of your content is video today?
